title: Platform Overview
desc: Introduction to SafeFood AI and the manager role.
contentHtml:
SafeFood AI is an enterprise-grade food safety management platform that combines automated temperature tracking with human oversight to ensure compliance and quality.


Your Role as a Manager

  • Monitor: Watch live temperature sensors and respond to AI-generated risk alerts in real-time.
  • Intervene: Issue warnings to staff, pause production lines, or escalate safety incidents when policy violations occur.
  • Document: Record incidents with timestamps, photos, and detailed notes for health inspectors.
  • Support: Assist kitchen staff experiencing technical difficulties with digital checklists.

Key Metric: Our AI detects 94.7% of critical control point deviations automatically. Your role is to verify the remaining edge cases that require human judgment.

title: AI Safety Engine
desc: Understanding hygiene detection, inventory tracking, and ambient analysis.
contentHtml:
SafeFood uses advanced IoT sensors and computer vision to automatically detect food safety risks. The AI engine runs inference models both locally on kitchen devices and on our cloud servers.


clean_hands Hygiene Detection

Ensures staff wash hands properly. Detects missing gloves, hairnets, and uniform violations.

ac_unit Temp Tracking

Tracks refrigeration and cooking temperatures. Triggers alerts when items fall into the danger zone for prolonged periods.

warning Cross-Contamination

Monitors prep areas for improper handling of raw meats and ready-to-eat foods to prevent bacterial spread.

inventory_2 Inventory Expiry

Detects expired ingredients and improper FIFO (First-In, First-Out) rotation using smart label scanning.

title: Live Monitoring Dashboard
desc: Navigating the kitchen control center interface.
contentHtml:
The Live Monitoring Dashboard is your primary workspace. It provides a real-time grid of all active kitchen zones with AI-generated risk indicators.


Dashboard Layout

Zone Grid: Shows up to 16 kitchen camera feeds simultaneously. Zones flagged by AI are highlighted with colored borders (yellow = medium risk, red = high risk).
Alert Panel: Right sidebar showing real-time AI flags with severity levels, timestamps, and one-click action buttons.
KPI Bar: Top metrics showing active staff, incident count, compliance rate, and AI confidence scores.

title: Manager Actions & Controls
desc: Sending alerts, halting prep, and escalating issues.
contentHtml:
As a manager, your role is to intervene when the AI flags an event that requires human judgment. Always follow the escalation ladder.


warning Send Alert

Sends a notification to the prep station's digital display reminding them of protocols. Use for first-time minor infractions.

pause Halt Prep

Requires staff to stop their current task and clean the area before proceeding. Use for moderate violations like spilled allergens.

block Discard Batch

Immediately mandates the disposal of potentially contaminated food. Reserved for severe temperature or hygiene violations.

support_agent Escalate to Corporate

Transfers the incident report to regional management for complex compliance or vendor-related issues.

title: Interactive Monitoring Sim
desc: Practice monitoring a simulated kitchen and safely intervening.
contentHtml:
This is a safe sandbox environment. The station above is a simulated recording. Practice clicking on the action buttons to issue alerts or log compliance.


Task: The simulated staff member left the cooler door open for too long. Issue a warning alert to demonstrate proper protocol.

Tip: In real shifts, always review at least 15 seconds of video context before issuing a warning. The AI provides confidence scores to help you decide.

title: Incident Investigation
desc: Reviewing AI flags and building HACCP logs.
contentHtml:
When the AI flags an incident, you must investigate before taking action. Follow this structured approach:


1
Review the Video Clip: Watch the 30-second buffer around the flagged moment. Look for context like recent sanitation.
2
Check AI Confidence: Review the confidence score. Scores above 85% are highly reliable for temp drops.
3
Cross-Reference History: Check if this station has previous flags. Equipment malfunctions show repeat patterns.
4
Document Your Decision: Whether you dismiss or escalate, record your corrective actions in the HACCP log.

title: Equipment Issue Handling
desc: Handling sensor failures, recalibration, and manual overrides.
contentHtml:
Not all flags are safety violations. Internet drops, sensor failures, and software glitches happen. Your role is to distinguish technical issues from real risks.


Manual Override

For verified sensor issues: take temperature with a handheld probe, manually enter the log, and mute the alarm.

Request Maintenance

If equipment failure causes significant temperature variance, submit a work order through the Hub. Relocate food immediately.

title: Compliance & Best Practices
desc: FDA guidelines, health codes, and maintaining audit trails.
contentHtml:
Food safety involves significant responsibility. Public health depends on accurate, unbiased monitoring. Follow these principles:


Principle 1: Safety First
When in doubt, throw it out. AI flags are indicators, but human judgment ensures absolute safety.
Principle 2: Consistent Standards
Apply the same HACCP standards during every shift, regardless of rush hours or staffing levels.
Principle 3: Traceability
Record every corrective action. Your digital logs will be reviewed during FDA and local health inspections.
Principle 4: Data Integrity
Never falsify temperature logs or override alerts without physical verification. Doing so violates health codes.
